prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

Extract Bioschemas JSON-LD for this training item.

Your prior analysis of this item:
## Metadata Notes - carpentries-incubator/better-research-software

**Type:** TrainingMaterial (indicated by context hint)

**Title:** better-research-software (appears to be the name of the material)

**Description:** This is a collection of materials related to improving research software. Topics covered include FAIR data, data management, metadata management, and various computational tools (Python, R, Bioconductors). It targets researchers and data professionals with a focus on making research more reproducible and accessible.

**Authors/Instructors:** Not directly listed. Content providers are listed (Elixir TeSS, ESRF, PaN Learning, CERIC-ERIC, etc. - a long list).

**Dates:** Absent. (Filter options suggest materials are being added/modified, but no specific dates given.)

**Location/Mode:** Online (part of PaN-Training platform)

**Scientific Topics/Keywords:** FAIR data, Data management, Metadata management, Python, R, Bioinformatics, Data science, Research Data Management, Open Science, Machine learning, Genomics, Statistics, Data visualization. (Extensive list available - see source)

**Educational Level/Target Audience:** Beginner to Advanced. Target audience includes PhD student

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

response_preview:
## Metadata Notes: vib-training-conferences/rdm-introductory-course

**Type:** TrainingMaterial (explicitly stated in the item name)

**Title:** RDM Introductory Course (inferred from filtering options)

**Description:** This is an introductory course on Research Data Management (RDM). It covers topics like FAIR data, data management planning, metadata management, and data stewardship.  The course is part of the PaN-Training materials and seems to be geared towards building foundational knowledge in RDM principles.

**Authors/Instructors:** Not explicitly listed. Content providers include Elixir TeSS, ESRF, PaN Learning, CERIC-ERIC and others (see full list on the page) but individual instructors are not named.

**Dates:** Absent. This appears to be a static training material, not a scheduled event.

**Location/Mode:** Online (material accessible via a website)

**Scientific Topics/Keywords:** Research Data Management (RDM), FAIR data, Data management, Metadata management, Data stewardship, Data management planning, FAIR principles, Data sharing. Also many related terms: Python, bioinformatics, cloud computing, statistics, genomics, etc. (a very long list provided on the page).

**Educational Level & Target Audience:** Beginner, PhD students, Researchers, Data Managers, Data Scientists, Scientific community. More specific targets include beamtime users and researchers.

**License:** License Not Specified (most common option on the page, but also Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International is present)

**Language:** Not specified (assumed English based on content)

**Other Fields:**

*   **Content Provider:**  Elixir TeSS, ESRF, PaN Learning, CERIC-ERIC, ExPaNDS, and many others.
*   **Tooling:** RStudio, RDMkit, BioContainers, Bioconductor, DESeq, and many other tools are mentioned.
*   **Standard Database/Policy:** European Nucleotide Archive, FAIRsharing, RDA DMP Common Standard.
*   **Difficulty Level:** Not Specified, Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced.

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,

## mTeSS-X: Scaling Training Portal Federation for RIs through Multi-Tenanting and Exchange - Poster 2nd OSCARS AGM 2026

[View material](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18773076)

With the goal of overcoming the fragmentation of training resources across Research Infrastructures (RIs) and the Science Clusters, the mTeSS-X project will enhance the existing open-source TeSS platform - developed by ELIXIR specifically for building aggregator training portals, and currently used in ELIXIR and PaNOSC training portals - to natively support federation. Such a fully-featured open-source multi-tenanted training platform is expected to be an innovation for building a federation of portals to help break down barriers between thematic communities, promote a more cohesive European research community, and promote FAIR and open training.

The project strives to support the federation of training catalogues using a multi-tenancy approach, and enabling cross-instance content exchange. This will allow RIs and their communities to maintain tailored catalogues with distinct identities, while simultaneously benefiting from a shared global pool of resources.

 **DOI:** 10.5281/zenodo.18773076

 **Licence:** Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International

 **Keywords:** TeSS, TeSSHub, training catalogue, OSCARS, mTeSS-X

 **Resource type:** Poster

 **Status:** Active

 **Authors:** Oliver Knodel (HZDR)

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

response_preview:
## Metadata Notes - GenomicsAotearoa/data-management-resources

**Type:** LearningResource (specifically, a collection of training materials)

**Title:** Materials - PaN Training

**Description:** This page is a directory of training materials hosted on the PaN platform. It provides access to resources covering a broad range of topics related to research data management, bioinformatics, and data science. Users can filter resources by date added, scientific topic, tool, standard database/policy, content provider, keyword, and difficulty level. The materials cater to various audiences including researchers, data scientists, and students.

**Authors/Instructors:** Multiple content providers listed, including Elixir TeSS, ESRF, PaN Learning, CERIC-ERIC, ExPaNDS, and many others. No individual authors or ORCIDs are explicitly listed.

**Dates:** Not specified. Filtering by "date added" exists, but no overall creation/modification date is provided.

**Location/Mode:** Online (platform-based)

**Scientific Topics/Keywords:** Extremely broad range including: Data Management, FAIR Data, Research Data Management, Python, Bioinformatics, Genomics, Statistics, Machine Learning, Cloud Computing, Data Mining, R, Data Visualization. Many specialized areas (RNA-Seq, crystallography, etc.) are also included.

**Educational Level/Target Audience:** Beginner to Advanced. Target audiences include researchers, PhD students, data scientists, data managers, bioinformaticians, and others in the scientific community.

**License:** Predominantly "License Not Specified", but includes some resources with Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International, MIT, and other licenses.

**Language:** Not specified (presumably English based on content).

**Other fields:**
*   **Platform:** PaN-Training
*   **Filtering options:** Extensive filtering capabilities provided.
*   **Tools mentioned:** RStudio, Data Stewardship Wizard, Bioconductor, etc.
*   **Standards/Policies:** FAIR sharing, RDA DMP Common Standard.

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

Critically review the following Bioschemas JSON-LD and improve it.
Focus on: completeness of metadata fields, correct @type, accurate keywords array, valid courseMode values, ORCID in author/@id when available, ISO 8601 dates.
Return the complete improved JSON-LD object.

response_preview:
## Metadata Notes - SciLifeLab-Training/open-science

**Type:** TrainingMaterial (explicitly stated in item name)

**Title:** Materials - PaN Training (appears as heading)

**Description:** This page is a catalog of training materials available through PaN-Training. It provides filtering options based on scientific topic, tool, standard database, content provider, keyword, difficulty level, and license. The materials cover a broad range of topics including FAIR data, data management, Python, bioinformatics, machine learning, and various research data-related areas.  It serves as a central repository for learning resources within the PaN community.

**Authors/Instructors:** Multiple content providers are listed (Elixir TeSS, ESRF, PaN Learning, etc.) but no specific author/instructor names are apparent.

**Dates:** Dates are not present on this page. It appears to be a persistent resource listing.

**Location/Mode:** Online (accessible via URL, no physical location mentioned)

**Scientific Topics/Keywords:** Very extensive list including: FAIR data, Data Management, Metadata Management, Research Data Management, Python, Bioinformatics, Machine Learning, Open Science, Cloud Computing, Statistics, Genomics, Data Mining, Data Visualization.  Many more listed on the page.

**Educational Level & Target Audience:** Difficulty levels: Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced, Not specified. Target audiences include: PhD students, Researchers, Bioinformaticians, Data Managers, Data Scientists, Scientists, and members of the PaN community.

**License:** Multiple licenses are represented in the materials listed: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International, MIT License, Apache License 2.0, and many others. "License Not Specified" is also a frequent option.

**Language:** Not explicitly stated, but likely English (based on content).

**Other Fields:**
*   **Content Provider:**  List of organizations providing materials.
*   **Tool:** List of software and tools associated with materials (RStudio, Bioconductor, etc.).
*   **Standard Database/Policy:** Listing of relevant databases and policies.
*   **Filtering/Sorting options:** Extensive filtering and sorting options available on the page.

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

Extract Bioschemas JSON-LD for this training item.

Your prior analysis of this item:
## Metadata Notes - SciLifeLab-Training/open-science

**Type:** TrainingMaterial (explicitly stated in item name)

**Title:** Materials - PaN Training (appears as heading)

**Description:** This page is a catalog of training materials available through PaN-Training. It provides filtering options based on scientific topic, tool, standard database, content provider, keyword, difficulty level, and license. The materials cover a broad range of topics including FAIR data, data management, Python, bioinformatics, machine learning, and various research data-related areas.  It serves as a central repository for learning resources within the PaN community.

**Authors/Instructors:** Multiple content providers are listed (Elixir TeSS, ESRF, PaN Learning, etc.) but no specific author/instructor names are apparent.

**Dates:** Dates are not present on this page. It appears to be a persistent resource listing.

**Location/Mode:** Online (accessible via URL, no physical location mentioned)

**Scientific Topics/Keywords:** Very extensive list including: FAIR data, Data Management, Metadata Management, Research Data Management, Python, Bioinformatics, Machine Learning, Open Science, Cloud Computing, Statisti

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

Critically review the following Bioschemas JSON-LD and improve it.
Focus on: completeness of metadata fields, correct @type, accurate keywords array, valid courseMode values, ORCID in author/@id when available, ISO 8601 dates.
Return the complete improved JSON-LD object.

**Authors/Instructors:** Not explicitly stated on this page. Content provider is European XFEL.

**Educational Level/Target Audience:** research data scientists, PhD students, masters students, PaN users, beamline users.

**License:** License Not Specified

**Language:** Not specified (presumably English, common for scientific materials).

*   **Content Provider:** European XFEL
*   **Across all spaces:** true (suggests wide availability)
*   **Email Subscription:** Available (Daily, Weekly, Monthly)
*   **PaN-Training Support:** Funded by Horizon 2020, Horizon Europe, and DAPHNE4NFDI.
*   **Contact Email:** pan-training@hzdr.de
* **Version:** 1.5.1

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

Critically review the following Bioschemas JSON-LD and improve it.
Focus on: completeness of metadata fields, correct @type, accurate keywords array, valid courseMode values, ORCID in author/@id when available, ISO 8601 dates.
Return the complete improved JSON-LD object.

You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

Extract Bioschemas JSON-LD for this training item.

Your prior analysis of this item:
## Bioschemas Metadata Notes - Bayesian Methods

**Type:** CourseInstance (likely a collection of materials presented as a course or workshop - filtering by topic suggests this)

**Title:** Bayesian methods

**Description:** This page lists materials related to Bayesian methods. It's a filterable list with options to sort and filter by date added and a wide range of scientific topics, suggesting a collection of learning resources. The page is part of the PaN-Training platform. 

**Authors/Instructors:** Not evident on this page.

**Dates:** Not evident on this page. Filtering options include date added ("In the last 24 hours", "In the last 1 week", "In the last 1 month") but no specific event dates.

**Location/Mode:** Online (accessed via URL, part of a web platform).

**Scientific Topics/Keywords:** Bayesian methods, FAIR data, Data management, Metadata management, Research data management (RDM), Python, Bioinformatics, tomography, Biodiversity, Data mining, Pattern recognition, Machine learning, Neural networks, Open science, and many others (see full list in page content).

**Educational Level/Target Audience:** Not explicitly stated. The breadth of topics suggests a range from beginner

- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

Critically review the following Bioschemas JSON-LD and improve it.
Focus on: completeness of metadata fields, correct @type, accurate keywords array, valid courseMode values, ORCID in author/@id when available, ISO 8601 dates.
Return the complete improved JSON-LD object.

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

Extract Bioschemas JSON-LD for this training item.

Your prior analysis of this item:
## Bioschemas Metadata Notes - R program6

**Type:** LearningResource (likely a tutorial or set of training materials - inferred from "Materials" section of the website and the nature of "R program")

**Title:** R program6 

**Description:** This appears to be a training material related to the R programming language. It’s part of a larger collection of materials covering topics like data management, FAIR data principles, machine learning, bioinformatics, and high-performance computing. The material is filterable by date added and scientific topic. 

**Authors / Instructors:** Not explicitly stated on this page.

**Dates:** Date added filters are present (last 24 hours, 1 week, 1 month), but no specific start/end dates for the material itself. 

**Location or Mode:** Online (accessed via website)

**Scientific Topics / Keywords:** R, R program, FAIR data, Data management, Metadata management, Research Data Management (RDM), Python, Bioinformatics, tomography, Biodiversity, Data mining, Machine Learning, Cloud Computing, Computer Science, HPC, Data visualisation. (A very extensive list is present on the page)

**Educational Level and Target Audience:** Not explicitly stated, but likely aimed

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

Critically review the following Bioschemas JSON-LD and improve it.
Focus on: completeness of metadata fields, correct @type, accurate keywords array, valid courseMode values, ORCID in author/@id when available, ISO 8601 dates.
Return the complete improved JSON-LD object.

**Dates:** Not stated on this page.

**Scientific Topics/Keywords:** FAIR data, Data management, Metadata management, Research data management, Bioinformatics, Health and disease, Health informatics, Medical informatics, Swiss Transplant Cohort Study, Apps, Project Management.  (Many others listed in filters – see content).

**Educational Level & Target Audience:** Not specified, but likely intermediate to advanced given the topic. Target audience includes PaN Community members, Data Managers, Data Scientists, Researchers, potentially biomedical researchers.

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

Extract Bioschemas JSON-LD for this training item.

Your prior analysis of this item:
## Bioschemas Metadata Notes - HemaFAIR Lecture 11

**Type:** LearningResource (specifically a lecture/training material)

**Title:** HemaFAIR Lecture 11 - Inside the Swiss Transplant Cohort Study App Ecosystem, Quick Tour and Project Management Insights

**Description:** This lecture provides an overview of the Swiss Transplant Cohort Study App ecosystem. It includes a quick tour of the app and provides insights into project management related to the study. The lecture appears to be part of a larger PaN Training program. 

**Authors/Instructors:** Not explicitly stated on this page.

**Dates:** Not stated on this page.

**Location/Mode:** Online (implied as part of PaN-Training materials)

**Scientific Topics/Keywords:** FAIR data, Data management, Metadata management, Research data management, Bioinformatics, Health and disease, Health informatics, Medical informatics, Swiss Transplant Cohort Study, Apps, Project Management.  (Many others listed in filters – see content).

**Educational Level & Target Audience:** Not specified, but likely intermediate to advanced given the topic. Target audience includes PaN Community members, Data Managers, Data Scientists, Researchers, potentially biomed

and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research

and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)

Strongly recommended:

  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)

  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"

             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")

- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string

- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

## Bioschemas Metadata Notes - Data management FAIR data

**Type:** TrainingMaterial 

**Title:** Data management FAIR data

**Description:** This resource covers data management and FAIR data principles. It's part of the PaN-Training materials, likely providing guidance and tools for researchers. Topics include data stewardship, metadata management, and research data management. The resource appears to be geared towards practical application of these concepts.

**Authors/Instructors:** Not explicitly listed. Content providers include: Elixir TeSS, ESRF, PaN Learning, CERIC-ERIC, ExPaNDS, etc. (a long list is provided - hard to identify key authors)

**Dates:** Absent.  Filter options include "Date added" but no specific date range is given for this material.

**Location/Mode:** Online - part of the PaN-Training platform.

**Scientific Topics/Keywords:** FAIR data, Data management, Research Data Management, Metadata Management, Bioinformatics, Python, Data Science, Open Science, R. A very long list of topics is provided.

**Educational Level/Target Audience:** Beginner to Advanced. Target audience includes PhD students, Researchers, Data Managers, Data Scientists, Bioinformaticians, and the PaN community.

**License:** License Not Specified (most common option listed). Other licenses are present, including Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.

**Language:** Not specified (presumably English, given context)

**Other Fields:** 
*   **Content Provider:** PaN Learning, Elixir TeSS, ESRF, CERIC-ERIC and many others.
*   **Tool:** RStudio, RDMkit, Data Stewardship Wizard, BioContainers, etc.
*   **Standard Database/Policy:** European Nucleotide Archive, FAIRsharing, RDA DMP Common Standard.
*   **Difficulty Level:** Not specified, Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced.

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

Critically review the following Bioschemas JSON-LD and improve it.
Focus on: completeness of metadata fields, correct @type, accurate keywords array, valid courseMode values, ORCID in author/@id when available, ISO 8601 dates.
Return the complete improved JSON-LD object.

response_preview:
## Bioschemas Metadata Notes - Data management FAIR data Reproducibility

**Type:** LearningResource (training material)

**Title:** Data management FAIR data Reproducibility 

**Description:** This appears to be a listing page for various materials related to data management, FAIR data principles, and reproducibility. The page itself doesn't contain the materials, but offers filtering options to find relevant resources. Topics include Python, R, data stewardship, and various bioinformatics tools. Resources are geared towards researchers and data professionals.

**Authors/Instructors:** Not explicitly stated on this page. Mentions content providers like Elixir TeSS, ESRF, PaN Learning, etc., but not as authors.

**Dates:** Not present. The filtering options allow sorting by date added, but no specific event dates are listed.

**Location/Mode:** Online (accessed via a website)

**Scientific Topics/Keywords:** FAIR data, Data management, Reproducibility, Python, R, Bioinformatics, Metadata management, Research data management, Data mining, Machine learning, Open science, Data Stewardship, Data curation. Many other specific topics are listed in the filters (RNA-seq, genomics, crystallography, etc.).

**Educational Level/Target Audience:** Multiple levels, ranging from Beginner to Advanced. Target audiences include PhD students, Researchers, Data Scientists, Data Managers, Bioinformaticians, and the PaN Community.

**License:** Primarily "License Not Specified", but includes resources with Creative Commons Attribution 4.0, MIT, and others.

**Language:** Not specified, assumed to be English.

**Other Fields:**
*   **Content Provider:** Multiple organizations are listed as content providers (Elixir TeSS, ESRF, PaN Learning, CERIC-ERIC, etc.).
*   **Tool:** Lists tools like RStudio, Data Stewardship Wizard, FastQC, Bioconductor, etc.
*   **Standard Database/Policy:** Mentions European Nucleotide Archive, FAIRsharing, and RDA DMP Common Standard.
*   **Difficulty level:** Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced, and Not Specified.

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution
