prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

Critically review the following Bioschemas JSON-LD and improve it.
Focus on: completeness of metadata fields, correct @type, accurate keywords array, valid courseMode values, ORCID in author/@id when available, ISO 8601 dates.
Return the complete improved JSON-LD object.

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title

             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

**Description:** This lecture introduces the concept of federated analysis applied to FAIR data. It likely covers principles and practical aspects of combining and analyzing data across different sources while adhering to FAIR principles (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, and Reusable). prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

Critically review the following Bioschemas JSON-LD and improve it.
Focus on: completeness of metadata fields, correct @type, accurate keywords array, valid courseMode values, ORCID in author/@id when available, ISO 8601 dates.
Return the complete improved JSON-LD object.

## Metadata Notes - HemaFAIR Lecture 6

**Type:** LearningResource (specifically a lecture/training material)

**Title:** HemaFAIR Lecture 6 - Introduction to HL7 FHIR Standard

**Description:** This lecture introduces the HL7 FHIR standard. It's part of the PaN Training materials, likely covering aspects relevant to FAIR data principles and interoperability within the biomedical/healthcare domain. It's an introductory level material.

**Authors/Instructors:** Not explicitly stated on this page. (PaN Learning is listed as a content provider)

**Dates:** Absent from this page.

**Location/Mode:** Online (part of a collection of online materials)

**Scientific Topics/Keywords:** HL7 FHIR, FAIR data, Metadata management, Health informatics, Biomedical informatics, Data management, Interoperability, Research Data Management

**Educational Level/Target Audience:** Not specified, but keywords suggest beginner to intermediate. Target audiences include: PaN Community, Researchers, Biomedical Researchers, Data Scientists, Data Managers, Data Stewards.

**License:** License Not Specified (most common option in the filter)

**Language:** Not specified, assumed English.

**Other Fields:**

*   **Content Provider:** PaN Learning, Elixir TeSS, ESRF, CERIC-ERIC, etc. (many listed)
*   **Difficulty Level:** Not specified
*   **Tool:** Not directly relevant, but FHIR often used with APIs, data exchange tools.
*   **Standard Database/Policy:** European Nucleotide Archive, FAIRsharing.
*   This page is a filter/listing page *for* materials, rather than the material itself, so info is indirect.

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

Extract Bioschemas JSON-LD for this training item.

Your prior analysis of this item:
## Metadata Notes - HemaFAIR Lecture 6

**Type:** LearningResource (specifically a lecture/training material)

**Title:** HemaFAIR Lecture 6 - Introduction to HL7 FHIR Standard

**Description:** This lecture introduces the HL7 FHIR standard. It's part of the PaN Training materials, likely covering aspects relevant to FAIR data principles and interoperability within the biomedical/healthcare domain. It's an introductory level material.

**Authors/Instructors:** Not explicitly stated on this page. (PaN Learning is listed as a content provider)

**Dates:** Absent from this page.

**Location/Mode:** Online (part of a collection of online materials)

**Scientific Topics/Keywords:** HL7 FHIR, FAIR data, Metadata management, Health informatics, Biomedical informatics, Data management, Interoperability, Research Data Management

**Educational Level/Target Audience:** Not specified, but keywords suggest beginner to intermediate. Target audiences include: PaN Community, Researchers, Biomedical Researchers, Data Scientists, Data Managers, Data Stewards.

**License:** License Not Specified (most common option in the filter)

**Language:** Not specified, assumed English.

**Other Fields:**

*   **Content

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

# de.KCD Training Materials

Welcome! This collection of training materials was developed as part of the de.KCD project for those interested in cloud computing and RDM as a means of self-learning.

You do not need any advanced experience—just a willingness to learn. Some familiarity with Python and command-line tools will be helpful.

Feel free to learn at your own pace, and do not hesitate to reach out if you would like support or to collaborate.

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

Critically review the following Bioschemas JSON-LD and improve it.
Focus on: completeness of metadata fields, correct @type, accurate keywords array, valid courseMode values, ORCID in author/@id when available, ISO 8601 dates.
Return the complete improved JSON-LD object.

The platform hosts materials, workflows, learning paths, and events. 

**Authors/Instructors:** Not explicitly listed, but contact email: pan-training@hzdr.de

**Dates:** Absent - platform, not a specific event.

**Educational Level & Target Audience:**  Professional development/higher education; target audience is the photon and neutron research community.

**License:** Not explicitly stated, but materials are described as "open".  Likely a Creative Commons or similar open license for the content *within* the platform.

**Language:** German (initial descriptive text) & English (most of the interface and linked materials).

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

DALIA,...?

**Description:** EOSC Academy is a training portal for the photon & neutron community. It provides access to materials, events, workflows, and learning paths. The portal is supported by multiple EU funding programs (Horizon 2020 & Horizon Europe) and the DAPHNE4NFDI consortium. It aims to facilitate training and knowledge sharing within the research community.

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

**Dates:** Absent. No specific start/end dates are given.

**Location/Mode:** Online – the portal is accessible via a URL.

**Educational Level/Target Audience:** Researchers, scientists, and potentially students involved in photon and neutron research. Level is not specified, likely spanning beginner to advanced.

- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

Critically review the following Bioschemas JSON-LD and improve it.
Focus on: completeness of metadata fields, correct @type, accurate keywords array, valid courseMode values, ORCID in author/@id when available, ISO 8601 dates.
Return the complete improved JSON-LD object.

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

Critically review the following Bioschemas JSON-LD and improve it.
Focus on: completeness of metadata fields, correct @type, accurate keywords array, valid courseMode values, ORCID in author/@id when available, ISO 8601 dates.
Return the complete improved JSON-LD object.

# TeSShub

# Create your own training catalogue on TeSShub!

Spaces are customisable, community-managed sub-portals within TeSS, each with their own catalogue of training content. New spaces can be requested by communities who wish to present their own catalogue with their own identity, for example, the PaN-Training Portal or the Research Data Management community in ELIXIR. The request to pan-training@hzdr.de will be processed by an administration team. If the request is approved, the administration team will create the space for you. Further details about the governance of spaces are decided by the administration team of each TeSS instance.

To request a new space on TeSS follow the instruction in our [TeSS Platform documentation](https://elixirtess.github.io/docs/)

Existing training catalogues 📚 on TeSSHub.Helmholtz.cloud include [pan-training](https://pan-training.eu), [DALIA](https://dalia.tesshub.hzdr.de), and many more!

This TeSShub is provided by the [Helmholtz-Zentrum Dresden-Rossendorf (HZDR)](https://www.hzdr.de).

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

# TeSShub

# Create your own training catalogue on TeSShub!

Spaces are customisable, community-managed sub-portals within TeSS, each with their own catalogue of training content. New spaces can be requested by communities who wish to present their own catalogue with their own identity, for example, the PaN-Training Portal or the Research Data Management community in ELIXIR. The request to pan-training@hzdr.de will be processed by an administration team. If the request is approved, the administration team will create the space for you. Further details about the governance of spaces are decided by the administration team of each TeSS instance.

To request a new space on TeSS follow the instruction in our [TeSS Platform documentation](https://elixirtess.github.io/docs/)

Existing training catalogues 📚 on TeSSHub.Helmholtz.cloud include [pan-training](https://pan-training.eu), [DALIA](https://dalia.tesshub.hzdr.de), and many more!

This TeSShub is provided by the [Helmholtz-Zentrum Dresden-Rossendorf (HZDR)](https://www.hzdr.de).

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title

- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"

Strongly recommended:

- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string

## Bioschemas Metadata Notes - PaN Training Events Page

**Type:** CourseInstance (primarily - page focuses on listing events/training) and Course (potentially, as "Learning Paths" are linked)
**Title:** Events - PaN Training
**Description:** This page lists training events offered through PaN-Training, a portal for the photon & neutron community. Users can filter events by date, and view options for subscribing via email, calendar, or RSS feed. The events are funded by various EU and DFG programs.
**Authors/Instructors:** Not explicitly listed on this page. Email contact: pan-training@hzdr.de
**Dates:** Not present for specific events - the page displays filtering options *by* date, and options to show past events.
**Location/Mode:** Mentions face-to-face or online training opportunities (implied), but specifics aren't on this page.
**Scientific Topics/Keywords:** Photon science, neutron science, training, scientific computing (implied through workflows & learning paths).
**Educational Level/Target Audience:** Researchers and professionals in the photon and neutron science community.
**License:** Not specified.
**Language:** English (default page language)
**Other Fields:**
*   **Provider:** Helmholtz-Zentrum Dresden-Rossendorf (HZDR) - implied through email address and imprint link.
*   **Funding:** EU Horizon 2020 & Europe, DFG (DAPHNE4NFDI)
*   **Related URLs:** Links to privacy policy, cookie preferences, imprint, about page, registration, API documentation, source code.
*   **RSS Feed URL:** https://test.tesshub.hzdr.de/events.rss
*   **Version:** 1.5.1

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

Critically review the following Bioschemas JSON-LD and improve it.
Focus on: completeness of metadata fields, correct @type, accurate keywords array, valid courseMode values, ORCID in author/@id when available, ISO 8601 dates.
Return the complete improved JSON-LD object.

## Bioschemas Metadata Notes - PaN Training Materials

**Type:** LearningResource (training materials accessible at any time)

**Title:** Materials - PaN Training

**Description:** This page lists training materials provided by PaN-Training. The materials are available in various formats and cover a wide range of topics. Users can filter materials by date added or keywords. These resources appear to be self-paced and accessible on demand. 

**Authors/Instructors:** Not explicitly listed.

**Dates:** Not specified (materials are available on demand, no specific dates apply).

**Location/Mode:** Online (accessible via URL)

**Scientific Topics/Keywords:** Extensive list including: elixir, gmod, metadata, jbrowse, covid19, one-health, microgalaxy, prokaryote, ai-ml, plants, NFDI, jupyter-notebook, biodiversity, FAIR, RDM, chemistry, cheminformatics, botany, genomics (16S, illumina, nanopore), ontology, data lifecycle, data literacy, FAIR data, research data management and many more (see full list in page content).

**Educational Level/Target Audience:** Not explicitly stated, but keywords suggest a focus on researchers, data scientists, and bioinformaticians.

**License:** Not specified.

**Language:** Not specified (likely English, based on keywords).

**Other fields:** 
*   Filtering options by date added (24h, 1w, 1m)
*   Sorting options (most recent, last modified, title)
*   Privacy Policy link
*   Cookie consent information.
*   Part of a larger PaN-Training ecosystem (links to Playground, Events, Workflows, etc.)

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

Extract Bioschemas JSON-LD for this training item.

Your prior analysis of this item:
## Bioschemas Metadata Notes - PaN Training Materials

**Type:** LearningResource (training materials accessible at any time)

**Title:** Materials - PaN Training

**Description:** This page lists training materials provided by PaN-Training. The materials are available in various formats and cover a wide range of topics. Users can filter materials by date added or keywords. These resources appear to be self-paced and accessible on demand. 

**Authors/Instructors:** Not explicitly listed.

**Dates:** Not specified (materials are available on demand, no specific dates apply).

**Location/Mode:** Online (accessible via URL)

**Scientific Topics/Keywords:** Extensive list including: elixir, gmod, metadata, jbrowse, covid19, one-health, microgalaxy, prokaryote, ai-ml, plants, NFDI, jupyter-notebook, biodiversity, FAIR, RDM, chemistry, cheminformatics, botany, genomics (16S, illumina, nanopore), ontology, data lifecycle, data literacy, FAIR data, research data management and many more (see full list in page content).

**Educational Level/Target Audience:** Not explicitly stated, but keywords suggest a focus on researchers, data scientists, and bioinformaticians.

**License:** Not specified.

**Lan

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

Critically review the following Bioschemas JSON-LD and improve it.
Focus on: completeness of metadata fields, correct @type, accurate keywords array, valid courseMode values, ORCID in author/@id when available, ISO 8601 dates.
Return the complete improved JSON-LD object.

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution

Extract Bioschemas JSON-LD for this training item.

Your prior analysis of this item:
## Bioschemas Metadata Notes - Discover Materials (PaN-Training)

**Type:** LearningResource (as per context hint - materials accessible at any time)
**Title:** Materials
**Description:** This page lists training materials available through PaN-Training. Users can filter and sort materials by date added, keywords, and other criteria.  The materials cover a wide range of scientific topics and skills related to research infrastructure and data management. 
**Authors/Instructors:** Not explicitly listed on this page.
**Dates:** Not present on this page.
**Location/Mode:** Online (materials are accessed via a website)
**Scientific Topics/Keywords:** Extensive list provided as filters: elixir, gmod, metadata, jbrowse, covid19, one-health, microgalaxy, prokaryote, ai-ml, plants, NFDI4Chem, jupyter-notebook, biodiversity, NFDI, pedagogy, train-the-trainers, virology, Large Language Model, application profile, gai-llm, research data management, FAIR, RDM, eukaryote, terminology, apollo, botany, illumina, nanopore, Cheminformatics, Chemistry, DALIA, Data Life Cycle, Data literacy, Digitalization, ELN, Ecologist, FAIR data, Learning Resources, OER, Ontology, Open Educational Resources, Plant Phenotypi

prompt_preview:
You are an expert metadata extractor for scientific training materials, courses,
and events — including life sciences, physical sciences, and related research
and education domains.
Your job is to extract high-quality Bioschemas/Schema.org JSON-LD metadata from
web-page text.

## Output a single JSON-LD object

### For TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
- name: full title (string)
- description: 2–5 sentence description (string)
- keywords: array of lowercase keyword strings

Strongly recommended:
- url: canonical URL
- author: [{"@type": "Person", "name": "...", "@id": "https://orcid.org/..."}]
  (include ORCID in @id whenever available)
- license: SPDX identifier (e.g. "CC-BY-4.0") or full CC URL
- inLanguage: IETF BCP 47 code (e.g. "en", "de", "fr")
- audience: [{"@type": "Audience", "audienceType": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"}]
- teaches: array of learning outcome strings
- educationalLevel: "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced"
- learningResourceType: array, e.g. ["tutorial", "video", "slides", "e-learning"]
- about: scientific topics as DefinedTerms
  (use EDAM URIs for life science: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Bioinformatics","url":"http://edamontology.org/topic_0091"}
   use PaNET for photon/neutron: {"@type":"DefinedTerm","name":"Tomography","url":"https://w3id.org/pan-training/PaNET01203"})
- timeRequired: ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H" = 2 hours, "P3D" = 3 days)
- identifier: DOI URL when present (e.g. "https://doi.org/10.1234/...")

### For CourseInstance (training event / workshop)
Required:
- @type: "CourseInstance"
- name: event title
- description: 2–5 sentence description
- courseMode: array — use exactly "online", "onsite", or "blended"
- location: for onsite: {"@type":"Place","address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"...","addressCountry":"..."}}
             for online: {"@type":"VirtualLocation","url":"..."}

Strongly recommended:
- startDate, endDate: ISO 8601 datetime ("2024-03-15T09:00:00" or "2024-03-15")
- url: canonical URL
- organizer: [{"@type": "Organization", "name": "..."}]
- maximumAttendeeCapacity: integer
- offers: [{"@type": "Offer", "price": 0, "priceCurrency": "EUR", "url": "..."}]

## TeSS ingestion conventions
- keywords: use an array, not a comma-separated string
- author/@id: use ORCID URI when known (TeSS extracts ORCID by regex)
- about: EDAM is primary for life science; PaNET for photon/neutron science
- identifier: include DOI as full URL; TeSS deduplicates on identifier
- inLanguage: language subtag only ("en", not "English")
- courseMode: TeSS maps "online" → virtual flag; use exact values listed above
- organizer vs provider: for events, prefer organizer; provider is the institution
